without_it.dafny_error: function precondition could not be proved; upper bound below lower bound or above length of sequence

diagnosis.explanation: Bounds invariant is structurally required. Without it, Z3 cannot prove that sequence slice indices (1 and i) are within bounds for Seq#Take and Seq#Drop operations. Also needed to establish CountBetter's forall precondition (elements have length >= 4) over the sub-slice scores[1..i].

without_it.dafny_error: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path: ensures rank == ExpectedRank(n, scores)

diagnosis.explanation: Accumulator invariant is structurally required. It connects the loop computation (rank) with the recursive ghost function (CountBetter). Without it, Z3 has no inductive hypothesis linking rank to the spec.

without_it.dafny_error: this invariant could not be proved to be maintained by the loop: invariant rank == 1 + CountBetter(scores[1..i], myScore)

diagnosis.explanation: When CountBetter unfolds on scores[1..i_new] (where i_new = old_i+1), it produces Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(scores, i_new), 1), len-1) which Z3 must simplify to Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(scores, i_old), 1) (i.e., scores[1..i_old]). This requires (1) Take-Drop commutation: Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(s,k),n) == Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(s,n+k),k), and (2) Take-of-Take: Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s,m),n) == Seq#Take(s,n) when n<=m. Neither axiom exists in Boogie's prelude. The assertion provides the equality via Seq#Equal extensionality as a workaround.
diagnosis.smt_gap: No axioms for Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s,m),n) simplification or Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(s,k),n) commutation

boogie: axiom (forall s: Seq, m: int, n: int :: { Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s, m), n) } 0 <= n && n <= m && m <= Seq#Length(s) ==> Seq#Take(Seq#Take(s, m), n) == Seq#Take(s, n));
axiom (forall s: Seq, k: int, n: int :: { Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(s, k), n) } 0 <= k && 0 <= n && k + n <= Seq#Length(s) ==> Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(s, k), n) == Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(s, n + k), k));
proposed_axiom.test_details: Eliminates invariant maintenance error (id103). Remaining 3 Boogie errors are subsumption checks that also fail in the WITH-assertion BPL (Dafny handles them internally).

non_essential_elements.note: The lemma and its call are redundant when the slice assertion is present. Z3 can unfold CountBetter and derive the recursive step on its own given the explicit slice equality.
Removing just the call (keeping lemma definition and assertion) still verifies.
Post-loop assertion is not needed. After the loop, i == n (from loop condition and bounds invariant), so scores[1..n] == scores[1..i] is trivially true from congruence.

notes: The core SMT quirk in this problem is the missing Take-of-Take and Take-Drop commutation axioms for Boogie's sequence theory. When CountBetter(scores[1..i+1], myScore) unfolds recursively, it produces a nested Seq#Take(Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(...))) composition that Z3 cannot simplify to the loop invariant's Seq#Drop(Seq#Take(scores, i_old), 1). The Dafny-level assertion scores[1..i+1] == scores[1..i] + [scores[i]] works around this by triggering Seq#Equal extensionality, which forces element-wise comparison. Ghost variables introducing the same terms without asserting equality do NOT help (ghost_var_test failed), confirming that Z3 specifically needs the equality in the e-graph. The lemma CountBetterAppend and its call are redundant artifacts of the proof strategy — the assertion alone suffices because it provides the structural decomposition that CountBetter's unfolding axiom needs.
